---
title: "quarto-liveurl — Core Features"
format:
  revealjs:
    liveurl:
      button: true
      shortcut: "u"
      position: "right"
      width: "44vw"
      mode: "auto"
      openNewTabFallback: true
      rememberLastUrl: true
revealjs-plugins:
  - liveurl
---

## What is quarto-liveurl?

- Attach live URLs to individual slides
- Toggle an overlay panel with **`u`** or the link button (top-right). You can try it on the next slide! It's disabled on this one since there are no URLs attached.
- Panel sits beside the slide — it doesn't cover it
- Works in iframe embed mode or as a link launcher

## Single URL — header shorthand {data-liveurl="https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/revealjs/"}

Add `data-liveurl="..."` to any slide header to attach one URL.

Press **`u`** — the panel opens and loads the page in an iframe.

> **Note:** some sites block iframe embedding. Use the ↗ button in the
> panel header to open in a new tab as a fallback.

## Multiple URLs — block syntax

::: {.liveurl}
- [Quarto Presentations](https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/)
- [Reveal.js Docs](https://revealjs.com)
- [Quarto Extensions](https://quarto.org/docs/extensions/)
:::

Three URLs → **launcher list** in auto mode.

Click a label to load it in the iframe. Use ← in the panel header to
return to this list.

## Both slide and global URLs {data-liveurl="https://quarto.org/docs/guide/"}

::: {.liveurl}
- [Quarto Gallery](https://quarto.org/docs/gallery/)
:::

When a slide has URLs *and* `liveurl.global` is set, the launcher shows
two sections: **"This slide"** and **"Presentation"**.

*(No global URL is set in this file — see `example-sticky.qmd` for that.)*

## No URLs on this slide

This slide has no `data-liveurl` attribute and no `.liveurl` block.

The link button (top-right) is **dimmed** and pressing **`u`** does nothing.
Navigate to another slide to re-enable it.

## Persistent memory

::: {.liveurl}
- [Quarto Presentations](https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/)
- [Reveal.js Docs](https://revealjs.com)
- [Quarto Extensions](https://quarto.org/docs/extensions/)
:::

`rememberLastUrl: true` (the default) stores the last URL you clicked,
keyed to the presentation file path.

**Try it:** click one of the links above, close the panel, then reopen it —
the same URL loads automatically.
